title: hieracium pilosella subsp. leucotegeum
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. leucotegeum, also known as mouse-ear hawkweed, is a subspecies of perennial herbaceous plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium pilosella subsp. leucotegeum include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or infused in oil to create topical remedies for skin issues and wounds.
